rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/02/2022 for judicial economy. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Notice does not indicate it is a petition for special letters. Petitions require 15 days notice. Notice was not given to Leon Krakosian as indicated in the Request for Special Notice. Please give required 15 days' notice to Vicken Sonentz Papazian and Armenian Revolutionary Federation WUSA, Inc. (Probate Code 1202). 2) There is no underlining general petition for probate filed. 3) Petition page 3 #5 petition did not select a required box of (3) or (4) and (7) or (8). 4) Will nominates spouse then Simon S. Chouldjian then Elizabeth Chouldjian as executor. It appears both have priority over petitioner. 5) Petitioner attached unfiled documents as Exhibit. Please provide file stamped copies of Exhibit A. 6) Petitioner hasn't provided courtesy copies. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 7) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 8) Objectors Alice Anivian and Simon Chouldjian has not provided the required courtesy copy of the objections. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/02/2022 for judicial economy. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Notice must be re-done. Please also give required 15 days notice to Elizabeth Chouldjian and Marie Amoudjaian. (Probate Code 1202). 2) Petitioner has not filed Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147) 3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 4) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEITH H. HOPKINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petitioner alleges that the decedent's trust is the sole beneficiary of this estate under the terms of the decedent's will and first codicil. The trustee of the decedent's trust waived the petitioner's account. The decedent's first codicil includes the following provision: "I direct that the net proceeds from the sale of my real property at 20 Arroyo Way, San Francisco, when sold, be distributed as further set forth in Article 7, Paragraph B of the Keith Hopkinson Trust, established October 27, 2009 and the First amendment to said Trust, executed July 24, 2012." Article 7, Paragraph B of the decedent's trust, as amended, requires distribution the net proceeds from the sale of the Arroyo property to various family members including the petitioner and trustee, and two charities. 2) The petition has not been amended to address how additional distribution from the predeceased spouse's estate and trustee of the decedent's trust should be distributed. If the codicil should be read to require direct distribution of the net proceeds from the sale of the Arroyo property to the trust beneficiaries, they may be entitled to interest under Probate Code section 12003 and need to waive the petitioner's account as a devisee whose interest in the estate is subject to accrual of interest under section 10954(a)(2). 3) The estate cash on hand is not fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 11th Amended General Order which took effect on 04/25/2022 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD CHESTER PRATT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/15/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Examiner is unable to determine which entries are for ordinary or extraordinary fees. Many entries are simply titled "email to client", "telephone call with client", "email from client", "research" etc. Please explain each individual entry so the court can determine whether the entry is ordinary or extraordinary. Seperate timesheets that show the ordinary and extraordinary fees may be helpful. Please explain and provide the legal basis for why counsel is billing for preparing an account when there has been a waiver of account. Court may not allow requested extraordinary fees requests that cannot be determined to be extraordinary. 2) The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.35 I. 6) b.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/31/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCES RUTH HIGGINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the proof of service filed on 4/21/22 showed that a SECOND AMENDED Petition had been filed, when a Second AMENDMENT TO the petition was filed [CRC 7.3], the full disclosure required by Probate Code Section 9202(b) includes HEIRS, not just beneficiaries, the full disclsoures required by Probate Code Section 1064(a) were not made, although there were numerous securities inventoried but apparently no longer on hand, and it appears that cash was never invested in an interest-bearing account, as required by Probate Code Section 9652(a). When a decedent is survived by a parent and no issue, the parent is the heir-at-law, not the decedent's siblings.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELLIOT S EISENBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/7/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The proof of service filed on 3/7/22 (no courtesy copy of which was delivered to the examiner, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.) is incomplete at item 4a (date of mailing). Pecuniary bequests not made within one year after the testator's death bear interest. [Probate Code Section 12003.] The proposed distribution to the beneficiaries of the pecuniary gifts must be set forth in an amendment to this petition and must reflect the 7% interest accrued since 1/18/2022 until the date of the next hearing and also indicate the daily amount thereafter, until paid. The Supplemental I&A filed on 1/25/22 was not properly verified by the executor, and the attorney's statement about the bond was not completed. (The supplemental I&A was for a portion of the estate, not "all" of the estate.) The penultimate sentence in paragraph 33 of the petition is incomplete. Were all costs, including the Probate Referee's commission, PAID? Transferring cash to another bank is not a change in the form of assets. Selling real or personal property for cash is a change in the form of assets. What happened to the tangible personal property? Assets on hand are reported to be all cash (paragraph 34 of the petition). But the proposed distribution still includes tangible personal property. It is unclear what the $3,305,641 amount represents in the proposed distribution or how it was calculated. No receipt has been filed for the distribution of the piano to Rabbi Friedman. Courtesy copies of the amendment and proof of service of the amendment and of the petition and a revised proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/27/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAN OEHL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/14/22 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The Bank of America business checking account was not held by the decedent and her surviving spouse. Unless the corporate bylaws permitted assets to be transferred to the surviving spouse (or other individuals?), in which case, a copy of the bylaws must be filed, that account cannot be transferred by a Spousal Property Petition. The petitioner might need to file a petition under Probate Code section 850. And ex parte petition seeking appointment of a disinterested party to serve as guardian ad litem for the minor must be filed. [LRSF 14.11 B. and F.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/3/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE R. GENE GEISLER TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 28, 1992, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Probate 850 Petition, For Order Confirming Trust Ownership Of And Conveyance Of Title To Bank Accounts
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Court to determine whether venue is proper in this county. 2) It is unclear what the document titled "Second Amendment" is, which names Petitioner successor trustee. 3) Gifts to Larry Mesple, Forest Cassidy, and Bruce Gullett were revoked by a holographic instrument. No notice has been given to them. 4) A Leo Michael Yost claims to be Settlor's son, but Settlor denied that he was. Petitioner does not have any contact information for him. Court to determine whether a further search should be conducted. 5) Petitioner alleges that the safe deposit box is an asset of the trust. It appears that Petitioner is referring to the contents of the safe deposit box, which have not been described sufficiently. Petitioner also alleges that the safe deposit box was closed, and she does not know what its contents were. 6) The body of the Second Amendment states that "this date" is 1/21/03; however, one witness signed it on 1/20/03 and the other signed it on 1/23/03. 7) Respondent Judith Bailey has not been given notice (which must be personal notice). 8) Petitioner has now identified intestate heirs but has not provided proper notice to them. 9) The supplemental declaration filed on 1/13/22 was not verified. 10) Kevin Sullivan (or if he is deceased, Bob Heuslein), specific beneficiary, was not listed in the list of those entitled to notice and was not given notice. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 11th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE 1996 HERMELINA TAN GARGARITANO REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED DECEMBER 9, 1996
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 11th Amended General Order which took effect on 04/25/2022 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS S. CHURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/1/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7., 14.18 E., and 14.59 E.] Courtesy copies of the petition and all supporting papers and a proposed order must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 5/23/22. It also appears that no will has been lodged yet. The clerks have provided only an empty "will file". Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
